story.tasks:
- Task 1: Create Factories Directory Structure (AC: 9.6.1) - Subtask 1.1: Create `test/factories/` directory - Subtask 1.2: Create `test/factories/index.ts` barrel export file - Subtask 1.3: Add tsconfig path alias `@/test/*` → `/test/*` (if not exists)
- Task 2: Implement User Factory (AC: 9.6.2) - Subtask 2.1: Create `test/factories/user.factory.ts` - Subtask 2.2: Implement `mockUser(overrides?: Partial): User` - Subtask 2.3: Implement `mockUserDto(overrides?: Partial): UserResDto` - Subtask 2.4: Add JSDoc comments and usage examples - Subtask 2.5: Export functions from `test/factories/index.ts`
- Task 3: Implement Permission Factory (AC: 9.6.3) - Subtask 3.1: Create `test/factories/permission.factory.ts` - Subtask 3.2: Implement `mockPermission(overrides?: Partial): Permission` - Subtask 3.3: Add JSDoc comments - Subtask 3.4: Export from barrel
- Task 4: Implement File Factory (AC: 9.6.4) - Subtask 4.1: Create `test/factories/file.factory.ts` - Subtask 4.2: Implement `mockFile(overrides?: Partial): File` - Subtask 4.3: Implement `mockFileDto(overrides?: Partial): FileResDto` - Subtask 4.4: Add JSDoc comments - Subtask 4.5: Export from barrel
- Task 5: Implement Additional Factories (Optional) (AC: 9.6.5) - Subtask 5.1: Identify frequently used entities in existing tests - Subtask 5.2: Create factories for identified entities - Subtask 5.3: Follow same pattern: UUID generation, defaults, overrides - Subtask 5.4: Export from barrel
- Task 6: Refactor Existing Tests to Use Factories (AC: 9.6.6) - Subtask 6.1: Identify tests with duplicate test data definitions - Subtask 6.2: Refactor `users.service.spec.ts` to use `mockUser()` - Subtask 6.3: Refactor integration tests to use factories - Subtask 6.4: Verify all tests still pass: `npm test`
- Task 7: Create Factory Documentation (AC: 9.6.7) - Subtask 7.1: Create `test/factories/README.md` - Subtask 7.2: Add JSDoc to all factory functions - Subtask 7.3: Verify barrel export (`test/factories/index.ts`) exports all factories
- Task 8: Verify Factory Usage and Test Execution (AC: All) - Subtask 8.1: Run all tests: `npm test` (should pass) - Subtask 8.2: Verify factories reduce test code duplication - Subtask 8.3: Check import patterns work - Subtask 8.4: Verify override pattern works correctly

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
### AC-9.6.1: Factory Folder Structure Created - `test/factories/` directory mevcut - Factory files naming convention: `[entity].factory.ts` - Her entity için ayrı factory file - Barrel export (`test/factories/index.ts`) ile merkezi import
### AC-9.6.2: User Factory Implemented - `mockUser(overrides?: Partial): User` - Database entity mock - Tüm required fields default values ile dolu - UUID generation: `id`, `domainID` için otomatik - Reasonable defaults: `email`, `firstName`, `lastName`, `phone`, `isActive` - `Partial` override pattern - `mockUserDto(overrides?: Partial): UserResDto` - Response DTO mock - Response DTO structure'ını match ediyor - Sensitive fields excluded (passwordHash, deletedAt)
### AC-9.6.3: Permission Factory Implemented - `mockPermission(overrides?: Partial): Permission` - Default permission structure: `id`, `name`, `key`, `description`, `module` - Reasonable defaults: `key: 'users:read'`, `name: 'Read Users'` - UUID generation: `id` için otomatik
### AC-9.6.4: File Factory Implemented - `mockFile(overrides?: Partial): File` - Database entity - Default file structure: `id`, `domainID`, `s3Key`, `fileName`, `mimeType`, `size` - Reasonable defaults: `fileName: 'test.pdf'`, `mimeType: 'application/pdf'`, `size: 1024` - UUID generation: `id`, `domainID` için otomatik - `mockFileDto(overrides?: Partial): FileResDto` - Response DTO
### AC-9.6.5: Additional Factories (Optional) - `role.factory.ts` → `mockRole()` (if Role entity exists) - `device-token.factory.ts` → `mockDeviceToken()` (notification tests için) - `notification.factory.ts` → `mockNotification()` (notification tests için)
### AC-9.6.6: Factory Usage in Tests - `src/modules/users/__tests__/users.service.spec.ts` - `mockUser()` kullanıyor - Integration tests - `mockUserDto()` expected response'lar için kullanıyor - Duplicate test data definitions kaldırılmış
### AC-9.6.7: Factory Documentation and Examples - `test/factories/README.md` mevcut (usage examples ile) - Her factory file'da JSDoc comments - Barrel export (`test/factories/index.ts`) ile import kolay

doc.title: Epic 9 Technical Specification: Testing Infrastructure
doc.section: Data Models and Contracts → Mock Factory Interface
doc.snippet: Mock factory pattern with Partial<T> override, UUID generation for IDs, reasonable defaults, and entity vs DTO factories. Example: mockUser(overrides) returns complete User object with all required fields.

doc.title: Epic 9 Technical Specification: Testing Infrastructure
doc.section: System Architecture Alignment → Testing Infrastructure Foundation
doc.snippet: Test directory structure includes test/factories/ for mock generators. Factory pattern ensures DRY principle, consistency across tests, and easy maintenance when entities change.

doc.title: Epic 9 Technical Specification: Testing Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Factory pattern for reusable mock generators in test/factories/. Entities always mocked in unit tests, real in integration/E2E. Time/dates mocked for deterministic tests.

doc.title: Testing Strategy
doc.snippet: Factory functions with Partial<T> overrides pattern. Example: mockUser({ email: 'custom@test.com' }) returns User with custom email and default values for other fields. UUID generation ensures unique IDs per test run.

doc.title: Epic 9: Testing Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Factory functions in test/factories/: mockUser(), mockUserDto(), mockPermission(), mockFile(). Partial<T> override pattern, reasonable defaults, UUID generation. Usage: import { mockUser } from '@/test/factories'.

doc.title: Story 9.5: Test Coverage Reporting
doc.section: Learnings from Previous Story → Test Infrastructure Complete
doc.snippet: Coverage baseline established. Mock factories will help improve coverage by making test writing easier. Test execution fast (<1s unit tests). Factories should maintain this performance.

artifact.reason: User entity structure - fields to mock: id, domainID, email, passwordHash, firstName, lastName, phone, isActive, emailVerified, createdAt, updatedAt, deletedAt
Permission entity structure - fields to mock: id, module, action, description, createdAt, updatedAt
File entity structure - fields to mock: id, domainID, userID, filename, originalName, mimeType, size, s3Key, createdAt, updatedAt, deletedAt
Example test file to refactor with mockUser() factory. Currently has inline user object definitions that should use factory pattern.
Integration test with real database. Uses setupTestDatabase pattern. Should use mockUser() for seed data and mockUserDto() for expected responses.
Integration test database setup. Factories will be used alongside this for test data seeding.
E2E test suite. May use factories for seed data and expected response validation.

constraints.constraint: Factory functions must follow Partial<T> override pattern: { ...defaults, ...overrides }
All factories must generate unique UUIDs using uuid() or crypto.randomUUID() for ID fields
Entity factories (mockUser) must include ALL database fields; DTO factories (mockUserDto) must match response structure
Factory files must be in test/factories/ directory with [entity].factory.ts naming convention
Barrel export required in test/factories/index.ts for centralized imports
JSDoc comments required for all factory functions with @param, @returns, and @example tags
Existing tests should be refactored to use factories to eliminate duplicate test data definitions
Factory usage must not impact test execution speed (maintain <1s for unit tests)
Factories must provide reasonable defaults that reflect common test scenarios (e.g., isActive: true)
Path alias @/test/* should be added to tsconfig.json if not present for clean imports

interface.name: mockUser
interface.signature: export const mockUser = (overrides?: Partial<User>): User => ({ id: uuid(), domainID: uuid(), email: 'test@example.com', ...overrides })

interface.name: mockUserDto
interface.signature: export const mockUserDto = (overrides?: Partial<UserResDto>): UserResDto => ({ id: uuid(), email: 'test@example.com', ...overrides })

interface.name: mockPermission
interface.signature: export const mockPermission = (overrides?: Partial<Permission>): Permission => ({ id: uuid(), module: 'users', action: 'read', ...overrides })

interface.name: mockFile
interface.signature: export const mockFile = (overrides?: Partial<File>): File => ({ id: uuid(), domainID: uuid(), filename: 'test.pdf', mimeType: 'application/pdf', size: 1024, ...overrides })

interface.name: mockFileDto
interface.signature: export const mockFileDto = (overrides?: Partial<FileResDto>): FileResDto => ({ id: uuid(), filename: 'test.pdf', ...overrides })

tests.standards: Jest v30.x with TypeScript support via ts-jest. Testing pyramid: Unit tests (services/utils) > Integration tests (API+DB) > E2E tests (user journeys). Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA) pattern mandatory. Mock all external dependencies in unit tests using @nestjs/testing TestingModule. Coverage target: 70% global, 80% services, 100% utilities. Factory pattern for test data generation to ensure DRY principle and consistency. Test execution speed: <1s for unit tests, <5s for integration tests. All tests must be deterministic and idempotent.

locations.location: test/factories/*.factory.ts - Factory functions (new files to create)
test/factories/index.ts - Barrel export (new file to create)
test/factories/README.md - Factory documentation (new file to create)
src/modules/*/services/*.spec.ts - Unit tests (refactor to use factories)
test/*.e2e-spec.ts - E2E tests (may use factories for seed data)

ideas.idea: Test: Verify test/factories/ directory created and barrel export exists
Test: Verify mockUser() returns complete User entity with all fields. Test override pattern: mockUser({ email: 'custom@test.com' }) should override only email field.
Test: Verify mockUserDto() returns UserResDto without sensitive fields (passwordHash, deletedAt)
Test: Verify mockPermission() generates unique IDs and has reasonable defaults (key: 'users:read')
Test: Verify mockFile() and mockFileDto() return correct structures with file metadata
Refactor: Update users.service.spec.ts to use mockUser() instead of inline user objects. Verify all tests still pass.
Refactor: Update integration tests to use mockUser() for seed data and mockUserDto() for expected responses
Verify: Import pattern works: import { mockUser, mockPermission } from '@/test/factories' (barrel export)
Integration: Run full test suite (npm test) after factory implementation. Verify no regressions and test execution speed maintained (<1s unit tests)
